scope: SYBEORG only; no Radi / House of Meroe files, targets, drafts, accounts, or campaigns inspected or used.
researchMethod: OSM/Overpass/Nominatim public listing discovery across Iowa and nearby Midwest markets, plus direct homepage fetches for candidates with website fields. No outbound contact was made.

selectedLeads.diagnosis: Directory/listing traffic can show a phone number, but it does not give Zip's a controlled first page to explain location, repair intake, or what a driver should have ready before calling.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Starter repair call-path page plus local profile website-link cleanup; add real services/hours/photos only after owner confirmation.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Simple visible gap with a useful sample page; the ask is finishing the first tap from a public listing, not a full redesign.
selectedLeads.firstOffer: $699 starter first page or $300 profile/contact-path cleanup if they only want the listing fixed first.
selectedLeads.upsellPath: Verified service pages, photos/reviews reuse, appointment request form, call tracking for future ads.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: Warm rural road/service-slip layout with public-facts verification card and mobile call dock; avoids dark bay-board/right-form/fake-review patterns.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed 2026-07-08: distinctive cream/orange rural road/service-slip concept; sample-not-official banner and footer visible; phone CTA visible; verified public facts card present; no fake reviews/certifications/owner claims. Minor watch item: logo-like ZR mark is acceptable because sample framing is prominent.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was checking Dubuque-area auto repair listings and noticed Zip's Repair shows a public phone and Bernard Road address, but I did not find an owned website link in the listing source I reviewed. I made a small no-obligation sample page showing how that first tap could look with the call button, location, and repair-intake prompts up front: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/zips-repair/ — worth polishing this up with your real services and photos? If not, no worries; reply or say NO and I will not follow up.

selectedLeads.diagnosis: A no-site phone listing can lose repair callers who want shop context, location confidence, and a clear mobile call path before they commit.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: One-page repair counter concept after confirming services/hours/photos.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Same first-page gap as Zip's, but needs a more differentiated concept before outreach.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; needs unique service/local-history direction before outreach.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup QA because no mockup was created.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was reviewing Dubuque-area auto repair listings and noticed Slaats Auto Repair shows a public phone and Sinsinawa Avenue address, but I did not see an owned website link in the listing source I checked. A simple mobile page with your call button, location, hours, and repair-intake notes could make that first step clearer. Worth me putting together a no-obligation sample after confirming the right services/photos? Reply or say NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Homepage fetch returned 200 but only 114 bytes
No title/tel/form/mailto found in fetched HTML
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The listed domain technically responded, but the fetched homepage was tiny and did not expose a title or contact paths. This is a concrete public-path risk worth flagging carefully.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Helpful alert first: verify homepage output, title, and visible call/contact path; then offer a small repair-intake landing page if they want help.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Specific bug-style outreach is easier to accept than a generic redesign pitch.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; use exact domain-output alert first.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. Quick helpful heads-up for whoever manages McCarty's Autopro's website: while checking Quad Cities auto repair listings, I opened the listed site mccartyautopro.com and my fetch got a very small 200 response with no page title or obvious call/form/contact hooks. It may be a hosting/theme issue or just blocking my check, but it is worth verifying from a normal phone/browser. Want me to send the exact notes? Reply or say NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Title: Bea Day Plumbers | Quality Work, Fair Price | Iowa City, IA
Tel links and one form found
selectedLeads.diagnosis: This is not a needs-website lead. The nearest monetizable leak is a dedicated tracked page for one high-intent plumbing offer such as drain/sewer or water-heater requests.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Campaign-specific service landing page matching their existing brand, with call/form tracking-ready structure and owner-approved copy.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Plumbing economics justify focused landing pages; outreach can be complimentary and additive rather than critical.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; generic plumbing page would not meet quality bar against an active credible site.

realAssetsFound.title: Bea Day Plumbers | Quality Work, Fair Price | Iowa City, IA

selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; brand-specific manual visual review needed first.
selectedLeads.draftSubject: Iowa City plumbing landing page idea
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi Bea Day team — Majid with SYBEORG here. I was reviewing Iowa City plumbing search paths and saw your site is already live with phone/form routes, so I would not pitch this as a rebuild. The opportunity I noticed is more specific: a dedicated service landing page for one high-intent offer, like drain/sewer or water-heater requests, with a tracked call/form path for ads or seasonal search traffic. If useful, I can mock up a no-obligation page that matches your current brand and uses only approved Bea Day copy/assets. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

publicListingSignals: Website fetch returned 403 Forbidden to batch check
selectedLeads.diagnosis: The public listing has useful facts, but the website returned 403 to this batch fetch. That may be bot protection, but it is worth a careful profile/contact-path check so real prospects are not blocked or confused.
selectedLeads.recommendedOffer: Local profile + website accessibility/contact-path check; simple request electrical service page only if verified useful.
selectedLeads.whyThisShouldClose: Electricians get urgent/local calls, and this is a helpful verification note rather than a design critique.
selectedLeads.designDifferentiation: No mockup created; active-site candidate needs manual visual review/brand assets before design.
selectedLeads.visualQaNotes: No mockup created; verify in browser before outreach.
selectedLeads.draftCopy: Hi — this is Majid with SYBEORG. I was checking Quad Cities electrician listings and noticed QCA Electric has a public phone, address, and weekday hours listed. When I checked the website path, my fetch received a 403 Forbidden response. That may just be bot protection, but it is worth verifying that real mobile visitors can reach the site and call path cleanly. Want me to send the exact note/checklist? Reply or say NO and I will not follow up.

mockupsSkippedReason: Quality standard: skipped generic or insufficiently verified mockups; used helpful-alert/research-only drafts where a mockup would be premature.
